steps.title: Identify the subject and tense context
steps.content: The singular subject “the song” is coordinated with “had spent” (past tense) in a compound predicate. The blank needs a verb matching both the singular subject and past-tense context.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and tense rules
steps.content: In a compound predicate, verbs must agree with their subject in number and maintain consistent tense. B (was ranked) is correct: “was” is singular (agreeing with “the song”) and past tense (matching “had spent”). A and D violate tense (present forms in a past-tense context); C violates agreement (plural “were” with singular subject). That matches choice B — B is correct.
